name: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi
benefit: ₹6,000 per year in 3 equal installments directly to bank account
summary: Income support to small and marginal farmer families holding cultivable land.
eligibility: Not a government employee or pensioner above ₹10,000/month
how_to_apply: Register at pmkisan.gov.in or visit nearest CSC (Common Service Centre) or revenue office.

name: Ayushman Bharat - PMJAY
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefit: Free secondary and tertiary hospitalization up to ₹5 lakh per family per year
summary: World's largest health insurance scheme covering ~55 crore beneficiaries from poor and vulnerable families.
eligibility: Family listed in SECC-2011 deprivation database, OR
how_to_apply: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in, then get Ayushman card at empanelled hospital or CSC.

name: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
benefit: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover on death due to any reason, at premium of just ₹436/year
summary: Affordable life insurance scheme for bank account holders.
eligibility: Gives consent for auto-debit of premium
how_to_apply: Enrol through your savings bank or post office account by submitting the PMJJBY consent form.

name: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana
benefit: ₹2 lakh for accidental death / full disability; ₹1 lakh for partial disability — premium just ₹20/year
summary: Accident insurance scheme available to all bank account holders.
how_to_apply: Apply through your bank by signing the auto-debit consent form.

name: Atal Pension Yojana
benefit: Guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 after age 60
summary: Pension scheme focused on workers in the unorganised sector.
how_to_apply: Apply at any bank or post office where you hold a savings account.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
benefit: High-interest savings account (~8.2% p.a.) for girl child, tax-free, matures at age 21
summary: Small savings scheme exclusively for the girl child as part of Beti Bachao Beti Padhao.
eligibility: Account opened by parent/guardian for girl child below 10 years
Maximum 2 girls per family (3 if twins)
how_to_apply: Open the account at any post office or authorised bank with minimum ₹250 deposit.

name: PM Awas Yojana - Gramin
benefit: ₹1.2 lakh (plain) to ₹1.3 lakh (hilly/difficult areas) for construction of pucca house
summary: Housing scheme for rural households without a pucca house.
eligibility: Houseless household OR living in 0/1/2 room kutcha house
Listed in SECC-2011 with housing deprivation
No pucca house owned by any family member
how_to_apply: Beneficiary list is auto-generated from SECC. Approach Gram Panchayat or block office to verify.

name: PM Awas Yojana - Urban
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
benefit: Interest subsidy on home loan (up to ₹2.67 lakh) OR direct grant for EWS/LIG families
summary: Housing scheme for urban poor including slum dwellers, EWS, LIG, and MIG families.
eligibility: EWS: annual income up to ₹3 lakh
Family does not own a pucca house anywhere in India
how_to_apply: Apply online at pmaymis.gov.in or at the local Municipal Corporation / ULB office.

name: PM Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
benefit: Free LPG connection + first refill and stove, plus subsidy of ₹300 per cylinder (up to 12/year)
summary: Provides clean cooking fuel (LPG) to women from poor households.
eligibility: Adult woman from BPL / Antyodaya / SC / ST / OBC / forest dweller household
No existing LPG connection in the household
how_to_apply: Apply at nearest LPG distributor or at pmuy.gov.in.

name: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana
benefit: Collateral-free loans up to ₹20 lakh for micro-enterprises (Shishu, Kishore, Tarun, Tarun-Plus categories)
summary: Loans for non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises including shops, manufacturing, services.
eligibility: Has a viable business plan in trading, manufacturing, services, or allied agri activity
Not a defaulter to any bank
how_to_apply: Approach any public / private bank, RRB, MFI, or NBFC. Online via udyamimitra.in.

name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme
benefit: ₹300/month (40-79 years) and ₹500/month (80+) from central govt, often topped up by states
summary: Monthly pension for widows from BPL households.
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat (rural) or Municipal office (urban). Online options vary by state.

name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme
benefit: ₹200/month (60-79 years) and ₹500/month (80+) from central govt, state top-ups vary
summary: Monthly pension for senior citizens from BPL households.
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat or Municipal office; some states accept online applications.

name: PM Vishwakarma Yojana
benefit: Training stipend ₹500/day, ₹15,000 toolkit voucher, collateral-free loans (₹1 lakh + ₹2 lakh) at 5% interest
summary: Support for 18 traditional artisan/craftsperson trades (carpenter, blacksmith, tailor, potter, etc.).
eligibility: Self-employed artisan/craftsperson in one of 18 listed trades
Engaged in trade on family-based / unorganised basis
No similar credit-linked govt scheme loan in last 5 years
how_to_apply: Register at pmvishwakarma.gov.in via CSC. Verification by Gram Panchayat / ULB.

name: Stand-Up India
benefit: Bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore for setting up greenfield enterprise
summary: Loans for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs to set up greenfield ventures.
eligibility: Setting up a NEW (greenfield) enterprise in manufacturing/services/trading/allied agri
how_to_apply: Apply at standupmitra.in or directly at any scheduled commercial bank.

name: Begum Hazrat Mahal Scholarship + Shaadi Shagun (Minority)
benefit: Scholarship and one-time ₹51,000 marriage gift for girls completing graduation
summary: Educational and marriage support for girls from notified minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi).
eligibility: Family income below ₹2 lakh per annum
how_to_apply: Apply on Maulana Azad Education Foundation portal or state minority welfare department.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship (SC/ST/OBC)
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / Tribal Affairs
benefit: Tuition fees + monthly maintenance allowance (₹230-1200) for studies after Class 10
summary: Financial support for SC/ST/OBC students pursuing post-matric education.
eligibility: Family annual income within prescribed limit (₹2.5 lakh for SC, ₹2.5 lakh for ST, ₹1.5 lakh for OBC)
how_to_apply: Apply on National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in).

name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefit: Cash assistance ₹1,400 (rural) / ₹1,000 (urban) for institutional delivery in low-performing states
summary: Safe-motherhood scheme to encourage institutional delivery and reduce maternal/infant mortality.
eligibility: Delivers at a govt-accredited (or empanelled private) health facility
BPL or SC/ST in High Performing States; all women in Low Performing States
how_to_apply: Get registered with ASHA worker during pregnancy; benefit disbursed at delivery hospital.

name: PM Jan-Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
benefit: Zero-balance savings account + RuPay debit card with ₹2 lakh accidental insurance + ₹10,000 overdraft facility
summary: Financial inclusion mission to give every household a basic bank account.
eligibility: Does not already hold a basic savings account
documents: Aadhaar OR any officially valid document
how_to_apply: Walk in to any bank branch or Bank Mitra (Business Correspondent) in your area.

name: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi NREGA)
benefit: Guaranteed 100 days of wage employment per rural household per year (wage varies by state, ₹230-370/day)
summary: Right-based employment scheme guaranteeing unskilled manual work in rural areas.
eligibility: Adult member of a rural household
Willing to do unskilled manual work
how_to_apply: Apply for a job card at the Gram Panchayat. Once issued, demand work in writing.

name: e-Shram Card (NDUW)
benefit: Universal Account Number + ₹2 lakh accidental insurance, gateway to future social-security schemes
summary: National database of unorganised workers — domestic help, gig workers, construction labour, street vendors, etc.
how_to_apply: Self-register at eshram.gov.in or through any CSC.

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
benefit: Free soil testing and crop-wise fertiliser recommendations every 3 years
summary: Helps farmers improve productivity through data-driven nutrient management.
how_to_apply: Approach local Krishi Vigyan Kendra / agriculture extension office or register at soilhealth.dac.gov.in.

name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana
benefit: Crop insurance — premium just 2% (Kharif), 1.5% (Rabi), 5% (commercial), govt subsidises rest
summary: Insurance against crop loss due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
eligibility: Cultivating notified crop in notified area
how_to_apply: Loanee farmers auto-enrolled via bank; non-loanees apply via CSC, PMFBY portal, or insurance company agent.

name: Mahila Samman Savings Certificate
benefit: Fixed deposit of up to ₹2 lakh for women @ 7.5% interest for 2 years
summary: Small-savings scheme exclusively for women / girls.
eligibility: Woman of any age (or guardian on behalf of girl)
how_to_apply: Open at any post office or authorised bank.

name: Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY)
ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution
benefit: 35 kg of foodgrains per family per month — wheat ₹2/kg, rice ₹3/kg, or now free under PMGKAY
summary: Highly subsidised foodgrains for poorest of the poor families under PDS.
eligibility: Family identified as 'poorest of poor' by state govt
Landless agri labour, marginal farmers, rural artisans, urban casual labour, widow / terminally ill / disabled-headed households
how_to_apply: Approach the local Fair Price Shop / Food & Civil Supplies Dept; eligibility based on state survey.

name: PM Kisan Maan-Dhan Yojana
benefit: Monthly pension of ₹3,000 after age 60 for small/marginal farmers
summary: Voluntary contributory pension scheme for small/marginal farmers.
eligibility: Small or marginal farmer (up to 2 hectares)
Not covered under other social security like EPFO, ESIC, NPS
how_to_apply: Register at nearest CSC or maandhan.in.
